title: The Dandelion
content:
O dandelion, rich and haughty,
        King of village flowers!
        Each day is coronation time,
        You have no humble hours.
        I like to see you bring a troop
        To beat the blue-grass spears,
        To scorn the lawn-mower that would be
        Like fate's triumphant shears.
        Your yellow heads are cut away,
        It seems your reign is o'er.
        By noon you raise a sea of stars
        More golden than before.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Vachel Lindsay, titled "The Dandelion", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
